events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need produce JSON answer. Need assess scenario: worn sheave grooves (belt riding low - ID). Field service, rough-complete. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. We need decide pass/fail. Worn sheave grooves causing belt riding low is defective, out of tolerance. Decision fail. Risk? Worn sheaves can cause belt slippage, reduced efficiency, premature belt failure, maybe high? In machinery, worn sheave grooves beyond manufacturer allowable wear (typically groove wear > 1/32 inch or belt bottoming) is fail. Risk medium or high. Since operating asset, belt riding low indicates slippage and heat; could be high if failure imminent. But as installed-defective, maybe medium. Need fill fields.

Need include component_conditions: installed-defective, worn, degraded maybe. Non-compliant? Since out of tolerance, non-compliant applies. Use list from allowed? The schema example includes installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. We can include installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded. Not failed maybe.

Value: numeric reading or computed quantity. We can estimate groove wear e.g. belt sits 1/8" below sheave top, or sheave groove wear exceeds OEM tolerance (e.g. > 1/32 in or 0.8 mm). Need set value. Maybe value: 0.125 (inches) representing belt riding low. Or value as percent wear? The prompt says estimate or interpret measurable condition. We can set value to 0.125 (inches) and maybe in findings mention. Confidence high 0.9.

Percent_complete: rough-complete, maybe 75.

Sound_source etc not relevant, leave defaults? Schema has sound_source string, event_time 0.0, rate 0.0, order list, workable list. We can set empty string, 0, empty list.

Findings: "Sheave grooves are worn, belt seated below top of groove; exceeds manufacturer wear limit." Actions: "Replace worn sheaves and install correctly sized belts per OEM spec; verify groove profile and alignment." Rationale: "Belt riding low indicates groove wear beyond allowable tolerance, compromising power transmission and causing slippage/overheating; per mfr guides sheaves must be replaced when wear exceeds spec." References: "OEM service manual / mfr guides; SMACNA? Maybe not. Use mfr guides.

Need ensure JSON valid, no extra text.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant", "worn", "degraded"],
  "percent_complete": 75,
  "value": 0.125,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Sheave grooves are worn, causing the belt to ride low in the groove.",
    "Belt seated below the top of the sheave groove indicates groove wear exceeds manufacturer tolerance.",
    "Condition will cause belt slippage, reduced power transmission, overheating, and premature belt failure."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace worn sheaves per OEM specification.",
    "Install correctly sized and matched belts.",
    "Inspect and correct sheave alignment and tension after replacement."
  ],
  "rationale": "A belt riding low in a sheave groove is a measurable sign of groove wear beyond the OEM allowable limit. Worn grooves reduce the grip surface, increase slippage, generate heat, and can lead to drive failure, so the assembly is not within tolerance and must be corrected before acceptance.",
  "references": ["OEM service manual / mfr guides"]
}
